---
title: Getting Started (React)
---

import CodeWithPreview from '@components/astro/CodeWithPreview.astro';
import { LinkButton } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';

export const example = String.raw`
import React, { useState } from 'react';
import 'hover-tilt/web-component';

export function MyComponent() {
  const [msg, setMsg] = useState('Tilt me!');
  
  return (
    <>
      <hover-tilt tilt-factor="1.5" scale-factor="1.1" shadow>
        <div className="card">
          <h1>{msg}</h1>
        </div>
      </hover-tilt>

      <input
        value={msg}
        onChange={(event) => setMsg(event.target.value)}
        placeholder="Type a message..."
      />
    </>

);
}
`;

#### Import the Web Component and use

Here's an example using the `<hover-tilt>` web component directly in your React app, binding the content to a state variable.

<CodeWithPreview
  lang="tsx"
  title="MyComponent.tsx"
  code={example}
>
